title: Homestead End Table
summary: A small end table with angled 2x4 legs, a thick 2x8 top, and an open bottom shelf — sturdy enough to double as a stool.
description:
This is the smallest piece in the Homestead Collection, alongside the Homestead Bench and Homestead Console Table already in this catalog — same 10° splayed-leg language, scaled down to end-table size, and built solidly enough that it doubles as a step stool or extra seating in a pinch.

The **top cleats and legs share the same 10° angle** as the rest of the collection, so a test cut and dry-fit before committing to the full set is worth the five minutes it takes — a 2x4 is cheap, a redone leg set after the top is already attached is not.

Pocket holes keep every fastener hidden inside the joint; if you don't have a pocket-hole jig, self-tapping structural screws driven from the outside work as a fallback, just less invisibly.

tools.note: Or a miter saw that bevels — every angled piece shares the same 10° cut.

cutList.note: Both ends 10° off square, long point to long point, ends NOT parallel.
Both ends 10° off square, long point to short point, ends parallel.
Both ends 10° off square, long point to long point, ends NOT parallel.
Both ends 10° off square, long point to long point, ends NOT parallel.

steps.title: Cut all pieces
steps.body: Cut the tabletop, leg, and shelf pieces per the cut list, paying close attention to which angled pieces need parallel ends and which don't. Dry-fit one leg assembly before cutting the rest.

steps.title: Attach the cleats to the tabletop
steps.body: Lay out the tabletop boards and center the angled cleats across the underside, leaving a consistent 1in overhang at each end. Attach with screws and glue.

steps.title: Build the leg assemblies
steps.body: Build two leg assemblies from the angled leg pieces, joining them with pocket holes to keep the fasteners hidden.

steps.title: Attach the legs to the top
steps.body: Attach the leg assemblies to the underside of the tabletop cleats.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: Clamp the bottom shelf boards in place between the legs and fasten from underneath with screws and glue.

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand every surface, then finish with stain to match the rest of the Homestead Collection.
